name: FMD analyses reported biological-age, immune-ratio, hepatic-fat, and metabolic marker movement
summary: A secondary/exploratory FMD analysis adds human biological-age and immune-metabolic marker support while remaining hypothesis-generating.
statement: A secondary and exploratory analysis reported that three FMD cycles were associated with reduced insulin resistance and pre-diabetes markers, lower hepatic fat, increased lymphoid-to-myeloid ratio, and a 2.5-year median reduction in a biological-age measure.
population_or_model: Adults in completed FMD clinical studies
time_horizon: Three monthly FMD cycles, with replication context from a second completed study
quantitative_note: The abstract reports a 2.5-year median biological-age decrease independent of weight loss.
caveats: The analysis was secondary and exploratory, not a prespecified durable clinical endpoint trial.
Biological-age and immune-ratio movement do not establish functional, disease, or mortality benefit.
The source reports diet-program sponsor and intellectual-property conflicts.
